_about: Canonical, controlled facet vocabulary for the difficulty auto-tuning system (see docs/designs/difficulty-autotune.md). SOURCE OF TRUTH: the add-to-backlog authoring step picks facet values ONLY from this file; the calibration/policy validate against it; it self-curates via the poor-fit gate. Facets are the join key that lets the policy generalise escalation history from past tasks to new ones. needs-human (gated) tasks are CARVED OUT entirely — no facets, never calibrated. The `work-type` + `risk` axes are UNIVERSAL (rarely change); the `layer` axis is PROJECT-SPECIFIC — the create-harness step tailors it to this repo's structure, and the poor-fit gate evolves it over time. ⚠️ The difficultyHint on each value is ADVISORY ONLY — human/LLM-readable taxonomy intuition; the policy NEVER reads it. Model selection is driven entirely by the outcomes ledger, the tier ladder (.tiers.ladder), and the floor/minN knobs (.policy). Do NOT wire difficultyHint into any code.

layer.desc: WHERE the change lives — inferable largely from the task's scope file paths. PROJECT-SPECIFIC: this is a generic STARTER set; tailor it to this repo at setup (and it self-evolves via the poor-fit gate).

data.desc: database schema / migrations / data access / models

meta.desc: docs / the harness itself / project scaffolding

work-type.desc: WHAT KIND of change it is — the main thing the authoring LLM reasons about. UNIVERSAL across projects.

risk.desc: Danger modifiers. A non-empty risk on a task forces the blocking audit (mandatory, bypassing the usual per-cell sampling decay) and clamps the starting tier to at least the ladder's second-cheapest rung, regardless of how reliable the (layer x work-type) cell has looked historically. UNIVERSAL.

touches-schema.desc: changes a DB schema / migration / persistent-data shape

touches-core.desc: changes a critical-path / framework / shared-runtime module

full-stack.desc: spans multiple layers (e.g. data + backend + frontend) in one task

policy._about: Difficulty auto-tuning policy knobs. The policy picks, per task, the CHEAPEST tier on the ladder whose historical first-attempt success rate for the task's (layer × work-type) cell is >= floor, requiring at least minN samples; otherwise it falls back to the authored difficulty (cold start). Bias is toward cheap — the escalation ladder is the safety net. poorFitThreshold = how many poor-fit signals accumulate before the add-to-backlog flow triggers a layer re-evaluation. exploreProbabilityPM (per-mille, default 0/off) = chance an eligible task probes one untested rung below the policy's normal pick instead — the ONLY way a newly inserted cheaper rung ever gets tried on a cell that's already calibrated to a pricier one, since it otherwise has 0 samples and can never become eligible on its own (see docs/designs/difficulty-autotune.md).

tiers._about: The GLOBAL difficulty ladder, cheapest → most expensive. The policy chooses a START tier from here per task; escalation walks UP it. The authored model/effort is the cold-start start tier. PROJECT-SPECIFIC — set this to the models your project actually uses (cheapest → priciest). The default is a deliberately SHORT 5-tier ladder — Haiku, then Sonnet low→medium→high, then Opus/high — so a stuck task blocks to a human after at most ladder_length × MAX_ATTEMPTS attempts (5×2=10). Extend the top (e.g. opus/xhigh, opus/max) ONLY if you'd genuinely want the hardest tasks built unsupervised — every extra rung is extra spend a stuck task grinds through before it asks for help. A rung's `effort` may be explicit `null` for a model with no effort parameter at the API level (e.g. Claude Haiku 4.5, the default tier-0 rung) — the loop omits `--effort` entirely for such a rung, and calibration matches it correctly (jq's null == null) without any special-casing.
